questions.q: Walk me through your resume.
questions.tips: Keep it to 2-3 minutes. Focus on transitions and growth, not every job detail.

questions.q: Tell me about yourself.
questions.tips: Structure: current role → key past experiences → why MBA now. Under 2 minutes.

questions.q: What are three words your colleagues would use to describe you?
questions.tips: Pick words that map to different leadership qualities. Have a specific story for each.

questions.q: What is your greatest accomplishment?
questions.tips: Choose one with measurable impact. Use STAR format: Situation, Task, Action, Result.

questions.q: Describe a time you failed and what you learned.
questions.tips: Show self-awareness. The learning matters more than the failure. Demonstrate growth.

questions.q: What do you do outside of work?
questions.tips: Show you're a well-rounded person. Community involvement and hobbies that reveal character work best.

questions.q: What would your direct reports say about your management style?
questions.tips: Be honest about both strengths and areas of growth. Reference specific feedback you've received.

questions.q: What is your greatest strength?
questions.tips: Back it up with a specific example. Choose something relevant to MBA success. Avoid cliches.

questions.q: What is your biggest weakness?
questions.tips: Be genuine — avoid disguised strengths. Show what you're doing to improve. Pick something real but manageable.

questions.q: What would your colleagues say is your biggest area for growth?
questions.tips: Reference actual feedback you've received. Show self-awareness. Describe your improvement plan.

questions.q: How do you handle stress and pressure?
questions.tips: Give a specific high-pressure example. Describe your coping strategies. Show that you thrive, not just survive.

questions.q: What skill are you most eager to develop in business school?
questions.tips: Be specific about the skill and why. Connect to your career goals. Show how the school can help.

questions.q: Describe a time your biggest strength became a liability.
questions.tips: Show nuanced self-understanding. Demonstrate adaptability. Explain what you learned.

questions.q: How do you solicit and process feedback?
questions.tips: Describe your feedback-seeking habits. Give examples of acting on feedback. Show growth mindset.

questions.q: What do people misunderstand about you?
questions.tips: Be honest and reflective. Show how you bridge the gap. Use this to reveal a hidden strength.

questions.q: Rate yourself on a scale of 1-10 and explain why.
questions.tips: Avoid extremes (not 10, not below 6). Be thoughtful about your reasoning. Show ambition to improve.

questions.q: Why do you want an MBA?
questions.tips: Connect past experience → skill gap → MBA value → future goal. Be specific about what you can't get elsewhere.

questions.q: What will you do if you don't get into any MBA program?
questions.tips: Show the MBA is a strategic choice, not desperation. Have a credible alternative path.

questions.q: What skills do you hope to develop during your MBA?
questions.tips: Be specific — avoid generic answers. Connect skills to career goals. Show self-awareness about current gaps.

questions.q: How do you plan to contribute to the MBA community?
questions.tips: Reference specific clubs or initiatives. Draw from past community involvement. Be genuine, not transactional.

questions.q: What is the biggest misconception people have about MBAs?
questions.tips: Show critical thinking. Demonstrate your research. Be thoughtful, not cynical.

questions.q: How have you prepared for the MBA experience?
questions.tips: Mention conversations with alumni. Reference relevant pre-MBA activities. Show intentionality.

questions.q: What alternatives to an MBA have you considered?
questions.tips: Be honest about other paths. Explain why MBA won out. Show thorough self-reflection.

questions.q: Why this school specifically?
questions.tips: Name specific professors, clubs, courses, and initiatives. Show you've done deep research.

questions.q: How will you contribute to our class?
questions.tips: Mention unique perspectives from your background, specific clubs you'd join/lead, and what you'd bring to study groups.

questions.q: Where else are you applying?
questions.tips: Be honest but brief. Show a coherent strategy in your school choices. Don't badmouth competitors.

questions.q: Why is this school your top choice?
questions.tips: Name specific courses, professors, or programs. Reference campus visits or alumni conversations. Show genuine fit, not flattery.

questions.q: What specific programs or courses attract you to this school?
questions.tips: Research the curriculum deeply. Connect courses to your goals. Mention unique offerings.

questions.q: How does this school's culture align with your values?
questions.tips: Reference specific cultural elements. Share examples of your values in action. Show you've talked to current students.

questions.q: What will you bring to the incoming class?
questions.tips: Highlight unique perspectives. Be specific about contributions. Connect to the school's community needs.

questions.q: Which clubs or organizations do you plan to join?
questions.tips: Name specific clubs. Explain why they matter to you. Show leadership aspirations within them.

questions.q: How does this school's alumni network align with your career goals?
questions.tips: Reference specific alumni outcomes. Show you've done informational interviews. Connect to your target industry.

questions.q: What do you know about our learning methodology?
questions.tips: Research the school's pedagogy. Explain how the method suits your learning style. Give examples from past learning experiences.

questions.q: If admitted to multiple schools, how would you make your decision?
questions.tips: Be diplomatic but honest. Show your decision framework. Emphasize unique school strengths.

questions.q: What are your short-term and long-term career goals?
questions.tips: Short-term: specific role/industry (2-3 years post-MBA). Long-term: broader vision (10+ years). Connect them logically.

questions.q: Why do you want to pursue an MBA at this point in your career?
questions.tips: Be specific about timing. Connect to career trajectory. Show you've explored alternatives.

questions.q: How will an MBA help you achieve your long-term goals?
questions.tips: Have a clear 5-10 year vision. Explain why an MBA is necessary, not just nice-to-have. Be realistic and specific.

questions.q: Where do you see yourself five years after your MBA?
questions.tips: Be specific about role, industry, and impact. Show a logical progression from your background. Connect to the MBA program.

questions.q: What is your long-term career vision?
questions.tips: Think 15-20 years out. Be ambitious but credible. Show purpose beyond personal success.

questions.q: Why are you switching industries/functions?
questions.tips: Explain the pull, not just the push. Show transferable skills. Demonstrate industry knowledge.

questions.q: How does your pre-MBA experience prepare you for your post-MBA goals?
questions.tips: Draw clear connections. Identify skill gaps the MBA fills. Show intentional career planning.

questions.q: What is your backup plan if your primary career goal doesn't work out?
questions.tips: Show flexibility without seeming unfocused. Demonstrate realistic thinking. Connect backup to your core interests.

questions.q: What impact do you want to have in your career?
questions.tips: Go beyond financial success. Be genuine about your motivations. Connect to personal experiences.

questions.q: How do you define professional success?
questions.tips: Be authentic. Show maturity in your definition. Connect to values and purpose.

questions.q: What industry trends excite you most?
questions.tips: Show you're well-read on your target industry. Explain how you want to contribute. Be specific and current.

questions.q: Who is your professional role model and why?
questions.tips: Choose someone in your target field. Be specific about what you admire. Show how they inspire your goals.

questions.q: What would you do if you couldn't work in your target industry?
questions.tips: Show breadth of interests. Demonstrate core transferable passions. Be creative but credible.

questions.q: Tell me about a time you led a team through a difficult situation.
questions.tips: Focus on how you motivated others, made tough calls, and achieved results despite obstacles.

questions.q: Describe a conflict you had with a colleague and how you resolved it.
questions.tips: Show emotional intelligence. Emphasize listening, finding common ground, and preserving the relationship.

questions.q: Give an example of when you had to influence without authority.
questions.tips: This tests cross-functional leadership. Show how you built coalitions and used data/persuasion.

questions.q: Tell me about a time you received critical feedback. How did you respond?
questions.tips: Show humility and growth mindset. Describe the specific changes you made as a result.

questions.q: Describe your leadership style.
questions.tips: Be authentic. Use examples. The best answers show adaptability — different situations require different approaches.

questions.q: Tell me about a time you had to make an unpopular decision.
questions.tips: Show conviction with empathy. Explain your reasoning, how you communicated it, and what happened.

questions.q: Tell me about a time you led a team through a difficult challenge.
questions.tips: Focus on your specific actions. Show how you motivated the team. Quantify the outcome.

questions.q: How do you develop talent in others?
questions.tips: Give specific mentoring examples. Show investment in others' growth. Describe your coaching approach.

questions.q: Describe a time you had to lead a team with diverse perspectives.
questions.tips: Show inclusive leadership. Highlight how you leveraged different viewpoints. Demonstrate cultural awareness.

questions.q: Tell me about a leadership failure and what it taught you.
questions.tips: Choose a genuine failure, not a humble brag. Focus on the learning. Show how you've changed.

questions.q: How do you build trust with a new team?
questions.tips: Describe specific actions, not platitudes. Show authenticity. Reference real examples.

questions.q: Describe a time you had to lead without a formal title.
questions.tips: Show influence skills. Demonstrate initiative. Highlight the outcome.

questions.q: How do you handle underperforming team members?
questions.tips: Show empathy balanced with accountability. Describe your process step by step. Give a real example.

questions.q: Tell me about a decision you made that was unpopular.
questions.tips: Show conviction and courage. Explain your reasoning clearly. Describe how you managed pushback.

questions.q: What leader do you admire and why?
questions.tips: Choose someone relevant to your field. Be specific about qualities you admire. Connect to your own leadership development.

questions.q: Describe your role in a successful team project.
questions.tips: Clarify your specific contribution. Show how you enabled others. Quantify the team's achievement.

questions.q: How do you handle disagreements within a team?
questions.tips: Show your conflict resolution approach. Give a specific example. Emphasize productive outcomes.

questions.q: Tell me about a time a team project didn't go as planned.
questions.tips: Take appropriate ownership. Focus on the team's recovery process. Share the lesson learned.

questions.q: How do you ensure all team members contribute?
questions.tips: Show inclusive facilitation skills. Describe specific techniques. Give an example.

questions.q: Describe a time you had to work with someone you didn't get along with.
questions.tips: Show professionalism and maturity. Focus on the working relationship, not personality. Describe the outcome.

questions.q: What role do you typically play on a team?
questions.tips: Show self-awareness. Demonstrate flexibility across roles. Give examples of different roles you've played.

questions.q: How do you build consensus when opinions differ?
questions.tips: Describe your process step by step. Show respect for diverse viewpoints. Give a concrete example.

questions.q: Tell me about a time you helped a struggling teammate.
questions.tips: Show empathy and initiative. Describe your specific actions. Focus on the teammate's growth.

questions.q: How do you handle a team member who isn't pulling their weight?
questions.tips: Show direct but empathetic communication. Describe escalation if needed. Focus on the team's success.

questions.q: Describe a cross-functional team experience.
questions.tips: Show ability to bridge different perspectives. Highlight communication skills. Quantify the impact.

questions.q: Tell me about a time you had to adapt to a major change at work.
questions.tips: Show resilience and agility. Focus on how you helped others adapt too.

questions.q: Describe a time when you had to work with someone very different from you.
questions.tips: Emphasize cultural curiosity and what you learned from the experience.

questions.q: Give an example of when you took a calculated risk.
questions.tips: Show your decision-making framework. Explain the upside, downside, and why the risk was worth taking.

questions.q: Tell me about a project where you had to use data to drive a decision.
questions.tips: Show analytical thinking. Describe the data you gathered, how you analyzed it, and the business impact.

questions.q: Describe a time you had to balance multiple competing priorities.
questions.tips: Show prioritization skills. Explain your framework for deciding what gets attention first.

questions.q: What's the most creative solution you've implemented at work?
questions.tips: Show innovation thinking. The best stories involve constraints that forced creative problem-solving.

questions.q: Tell me about a time you failed and what you learned from it.
questions.tips: Use the STAR method. Focus on the lesson, not just the failure. Show self-awareness.

questions.q: Describe a situation where you had to influence someone without direct authority.
questions.tips: Highlight interpersonal skills. Show strategic thinking. Quantify the outcome.

questions.q: Walk me through a time you received critical feedback. How did you respond?
questions.tips: Show humility and growth mindset. Describe concrete changes you made. Avoid being defensive in your retelling.

questions.q: Tell me about a time you had to make a decision with incomplete information.
questions.tips: Explain your reasoning framework. Show comfort with ambiguity. Describe the outcome and what you'd do differently.

questions.q: Describe a conflict with a colleague and how you resolved it.
questions.tips: Show emotional intelligence. Focus on the resolution process. Avoid placing blame.

questions.q: Give an example of when you went above and beyond what was expected.
questions.tips: Quantify the impact. Explain your motivation. Connect to your values.

questions.q: Describe a situation where you had to manage competing priorities.
questions.tips: Show your prioritization framework. Demonstrate time management skills. Explain trade-offs you made.

questions.q: Tell me about a time you took a calculated risk.
questions.tips: Explain your risk assessment process. Show that you weighed pros and cons. Describe the outcome honestly.

questions.q: Describe a moment when you demonstrated resilience.
questions.tips: Choose a genuinely challenging situation. Show the emotional journey. Connect to personal growth.

questions.q: Tell me about an ethical dilemma you faced at work.
questions.tips: Show clear moral reasoning. Explain the competing interests and how you decided what was right.

questions.q: What matters most to you?
questions.tips: Stanford's signature question. Be deeply personal and authentic. This is about values, not achievements.

questions.q: How would you handle discovering a superior was doing something unethical?
questions.tips: Show courage and judgment. Discuss escalation paths and protecting stakeholders.

questions.q: What social or community issue are you most passionate about?
questions.tips: Show genuine passion with specific action. Talking about an issue is weaker than showing what you've done about it.

questions.q: Describe a time you faced an ethical dilemma at work.
questions.tips: Choose a genuine dilemma, not an obvious right/wrong. Walk through your decision-making process. Show your values in action.

questions.q: What would you do if you discovered a colleague was being dishonest?
questions.tips: Show moral courage. Describe a measured approach. Balance loyalty with integrity.

questions.q: How do you handle situations where business goals conflict with ethics?
questions.tips: Show that you don't see it as binary. Demonstrate creative problem-solving. Reference your personal framework.

questions.q: Tell me about a time you had to speak truth to power.
questions.tips: Show courage balanced with tact. Describe the preparation involved. Share the outcome honestly.

questions.q: Describe a situation where you had to choose between two right answers.
questions.tips: Show sophisticated moral reasoning. Explain your decision framework. Be honest about the trade-offs.

questions.q: How do you ensure ethical behavior in your team?
questions.tips: Describe specific practices. Show proactive culture-building. Give a concrete example.

questions.q: What is your personal code of ethics?
questions.tips: Be specific, not vague. Root it in experiences. Show how it guides decisions.

questions.q: Describe a time you sacrificed personal gain for the right thing.
questions.tips: Choose a meaningful example. Show that you'd do it again. Connect to your core values.

questions.q: What trends do you see shaping your industry in the next 5 years?
questions.tips: Show thought leadership. Name 2-3 specific trends with evidence, and explain implications.

questions.q: If you could start a company, what would it be?
questions.tips: Show entrepreneurial thinking. The idea matters less than your reasoning about market need and execution.

questions.q: What business leader do you admire and why?
questions.tips: Choose someone whose values align with yours. Explain specific decisions or qualities you admire, not just outcomes.

questions.q: Tell me about a recent business news story that caught your attention.
questions.tips: Pick something relevant to your target industry. Have a nuanced view, not just a summary.

hbs.prep_tip: HBS uses case-based discussions. Prepare to analyze business situations on the spot.

gsb.prep_tip: Stanford emphasizes self-awareness. 'What matters most' is key — prepare deeply personal stories.

wharton.prep_tip: Unique group exercise. Practice collaborative problem-solving and showing leadership without dominating.

booth.prep_tip: Booth values analytical rigor. Prepare to discuss how you approach problems with data and frameworks.

kellogg.prep_tip: Kellogg prizes teamwork and emotional intelligence. Show genuine warmth and collaboration stories.

sloan.prep_tip: MIT values innovation and impact. Prepare stories about driving change through analytical thinking.

cbs.format: Blind interview (interviewer hasn't read your application)
cbs.prep_tip: Since the interviewer hasn't read your app, you need to efficiently convey your story from scratch.

tuck.prep_tip: Tuck emphasizes community. Show how you've contributed to communities and why small class size appeals to you.

haas.prep_tip: Haas's four defining principles (Question the Status Quo, Confidence Without Attitude, Students Always, Beyond Yourself) — weave them in.
